reviews.review_msg: Not approachable, very condescending demeanour. Zero for teaching method . Just because you have a phd it doesn't mean you are automatically a good teacher.

Not very approachable and can come across as condescending. Feedback is often unclear or dismissive. Exams and assignments felt confusing rather than focused on learning. Grading seemed inconsistent, and the final percentage did not match how grades were determined. This lack of transparency made the course unnecessarily stressful.

Her slides are very boring. She goes way too fast and will skip over the breaks if she feels like it. She does not provide practice questions, and relies entirely on the Teaching Assistants to teach the course. She'll fly through the important lecture content. You can do well in her course by attending tutorials and doing the readings.

Her slides are very boring. She goes way too fast and will skip over the breaks if she feels like it. She does not provide practice questions, and relies entirely on the Teaching Assistants to teach the course. She'll fly through the important lecture content. You can do well in her course by attending tutorials and doing the readings.

Travis is a fantastic professor -- material is challenging and he is a tough, but fair, grader (I nearly failed my final paper). Regardless, I learned an immense amount in his class, and have the utmost respect for him as a teacher. Incredibly inspirational, and got me interested in social epi!

Travis is fantastic. I had him for a graduate course in epidemiology (social epi mostly), and learned an incredible amount. The material is challenging (to me), but he made it very engaging, and my learning was immense. He is a tough, but fair, grader. Travis is the best!
